raw_data.details: The "ICMR Post Doctoral Fellowship (ICMR-PDF)" scheme implemented by the Indian Council of Medical Research, Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, aims to promote advanced research in health sciences. It provides opportunities to fresh PhD, MD, or MS holders to work in ICMR institutes and centres. The scheme focuses on research in areas like basic science, communicable and non-communicable diseases, reproductive health, and nutrition. Every year, around 50 fellowships are awarded to deserving candidates. Fellows get access to modern research facilities and expert guidance at ICMR institutions. The aim is to strengthen high-quality research and build skilled professionals in the health sector.
raw_data.benefits: Monthly Fellowship: ₹65,000 per month along with HRA and NPA as per Government rules. Contingency Grant: ₹3,00,000 per year for research-related expenses. Travel Support: Up to 25% of the contingency grant for national travel related to research work. Tenure Duration: 2 years, extendable by 1 more year based on performance. Refund Clause: Fellowship amount must be refunded if leaving before 1 year. Fund Disbursement: Fellowship and contingency grants are released every six months. Accommodation Facility: Accommodation may be provided (HRA not applicable if provided). Medical & Leave Benefits: As per ICMR rules for fellows.
raw_data.eligibility: ICMR-PDF is open to Indian nationals only. Fresh PhDs/MD/MS within three years of completing their Ph. D./MD/MS degree (awarded) as on the last date of the application and also those who have received their provisional Ph. D./MD/MS degree can apply. The Post-Doctoral proposed research work should be the continuation of the Ph. D. or MD/MS research work. If not, then the application will not be considered. The maximum age limit is 32 years, relaxable up to 5 years in case of SC/ST/OBC/PH and women candidates, as on the last date of application. For deserving candidates age can be relaxed maximum up to a maximum of 3 years based on earlier work done, publications, awards, etc. along with the request by the sponsoring Director/Director-in-Charge. Director General, ICMR may consider and relax on the recommendation of the screening committee. The PDF Guide should have the minimum designation of Scientist-C grade. Postgraduate medical students are also permitted to carry out ICMR’s PDF in MCI-recognized postgraduate medical colleges wherein a minimum of three batches have obtained their postgraduate degrees. Note 01: Result notification/certificate or Viva-voce/Thesis submitted notification will not be considered. Note 02: It is mandatory to submit the Plagiarism check report along with the declaration to be submitted to ICMR along with the PDF proposal and application. Note 03: A list of ICMR Institutes/Centers and their niche Areas/R&D activities are available on the ICMR website.
raw_data.application_process:
OfflineLast Date of Application (30th June and 31st December Every Year)
Step 01: Eligible candidates can visit the [official website](https://main.icmr.nic.in/content/post-doctoral-research)of ICMR and download the [application form](https://www.icmr.gov.in/icmrobject/custom_data/pdf/pdr/Download_Form_v1.doc).
Step 02: Fill out the application form completely and sign as required.
Step 03: No column of the application form should be left blank. Kindly write NA/Nil as applicable.
Step 04: Use additional sheets where necessary, with relevant columns drawn thereon, inserting proper references.
Step 05: The completed application should be accompanied by all the requisite documents and attach self-attested photocopies of all documents as required.
Step 06: Applications duly filled in the prescribed format and complete in all respects should be sent to:Director General(Kind Attention)Soft copy of the application to be sent on email atpdfhrd.icmr[at]gmail[dot]com.“Application for ICMR centenary post-doctoral fellowship” should clearly be written on top of the envelope containing application form. Note 01: Incomplete applications are liable to be rejected. Note 02: Applications complete in all respects must reach on or before the closing date (as mentioned above). Note 03: Candidates are advised to contact the Director/Officer-in-Charge of ICMR Institutes/Centers where they are interested in pursuing their PDF. Note 04: Applicants can visit the website of the particular ICMR Institute/Center to know the activities of the Institutes/Centers and their mandates. Note 05: Applications received without the recommendation of the Director /Officer-in-charge of the sponsoring ICMR institutes/centers will not be considered. Mode of Selection:The selection of the “ICMR Centenary Post Doctoral Fellows” will be made through the interview of short-listed candidates by a specially constituted Selection Committee by DG, ICMR based on their publications, citations, and impact factors. The place of the interview will be ICMR Hqrs. New Delhi. Eligible candidates must route their applications through the sponsoring ICMR Institutes/ Centers. Applicants coming from abroad may also be considered in absentia, if eligible and found suitable by the Committee.
raw_data.documents_required: Passport-size Photograph. Attested copy of School Leaving/ High School/Secondary School Certificate (for date of birth). Attested copies of Mark Sheets/Grade Cards & Degrees. Attested copy of the Caste certificate, if applicable. Reprints of publications. One-page summary of Ph. D. thesis. Attested copies of Letter(s) of Acceptance for paper(s) accepted for publication & pre-prints. Detailed research proposal. Testimonials from two Referees (One should be Ph. D. Guide). Plagiarism Check Report verified by ICMR-RIO. Documents Format: [Click here](https://epms.icmr.org.in/pdf-fellowship).

It provides opportunities to fresh PhD, MD, or MS holders to work in ICMR institutes and centres. The scheme focuses on research in areas like basic science, communicable and non-communicable diseases, reproductive health, and nutrition. Every year, around 50 fellowships are awarded to deserving candidates. Fellows get access to modern research facilities and expert guidance at ICMR institutions. D./MD/MS degree (awarded) as on the last date of the application and also those who have received their provisional Ph. D./MD/MS degree can apply. The Post-Doctoral proposed research work should be the continuation of the Ph. D. or MD/MS research work. If not, then the application will not be considered. The maximum age limit is 32 years, relaxable up to 5 years in case of SC/ST/OBC/PH and women candidates, as on the last date of application. For deserving candidates age can be relaxed maximum up to a maximum of 3 years based on earlier work done, publications, awards, etc. along with the request by the sponsoring Director/Director-in-Charge. Director General, ICMR may consider and relax on the recommendation of the screening committee. The PDF Guide should have the minimum designation of Scientist-C grade. Postgraduate medical students are also permitted to carry out ICMR’s PDF in MCI-recognized postgraduate medical colleges wherein a minimum of three batches have obtained their postgraduate degrees. Note 01: Result notification/certificate or Viva-voce/Thesis submitted notification will not be considered. Note 02: It is mandatory to submit the Plagiarism check report along with the declaration to be submitted to ICMR along with the PDF proposal and application. Note 03: A list of ICMR Institutes/Centers and their niche Areas/R&D activities are available on the ICMR website. Application ProcessOfflineLast Date of Application (30th June and 31st December Every Year)Step 01: Eligible candidates can visit the [official website](https://main.icmr.nic.in/content/post-doctoral-research)of ICMR and download the [application form](https://www.icmr.gov.in/icmrobject/custom_data/pdf/pdr/Download_Form_v1.doc). Step 02: Fill out the application form completely and sign as required. Step 03: No column of the application form should be left blank. Kindly write NA/Nil as applicable. Step 04: Use additional sheets where necessary, with relevant columns drawn thereon, inserting proper references. Step 05: The completed application should be accompanied by all the requisite documents and attach self-attested photocopies of all documents as required. Note 01: Incomplete applications are liable to be rejected. Note 02: Applications complete in all respects must reach on or before the closing date (as mentioned above). Note 03: Candidates are advised to contact the Director/Officer-in-Charge of ICMR Institutes/Centers where they are interested in pursuing their PDF. Note 04: Applicants can visit the website of the particular ICMR Institute/Center to know the activities of the Institutes/Centers and their mandates. Note 05: Applications received without the recommendation of the Director /Officer-in-charge of the sponsoring ICMR institutes/centers will not be considered. Mode of Selection:The selection of the “ICMR Centenary Post Doctoral Fellows” will be made through the interview of short-listed candidates by a specially constituted Selection Committee by DG, ICMR based on their publications, citations, and impact factors. The place of the interview will be ICMR Hqrs. New Delhi. Eligible candidates must route their applications through the sponsoring ICMR Institutes/ Centers. Applicants coming from abroad may also be considered in absentia, if eligible and found suitable by the Committee. Documents RequiredPassport-size Photograph. Attested copy of School Leaving/ High School/Secondary School Certificate (for date of birth). Attested copies of Mark Sheets/Grade Cards & Degrees. Attested copy of the Caste certificate, if applicable. Reprints of publications. One-page summary of Ph. D. thesis. Attested copies of Letter(s) of Acceptance for paper(s) accepted for publication & pre-prints. Detailed research proposal. Testimonials from two Referees (One should be Ph. D. Guide). Plagiarism Check Report verified by ICMR-RIO. Documents Format: [Click here](https://epms.icmr.org.in/pdf-fellowship). Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the “ICMR- Post Doctoral Fellowship” Scheme? ICMR- Post Doctoral Fellowship (ICMR-PDF) Scheme is being instituted to foster high-quality research opportunities for promising fresh Ph. D./MD/MS holders in the cutting-edge areas of basic science, communicable and non-communicable diseases, and reproductive health including nutrition at ICMR Institutes /Centers. Which is the implementing agency of this scheme? Indian Council of Medical Research, Government of IndiaWho are eligible to apply under the scheme? Fresh PhDs/MD/MS within three years of completing their Ph. D./MD/MS degree (awarded) as on the last date of the application and also those who have received their provisional Ph. D./MD/MS degree can apply. What is the number of fellowships awarded annually? ICMR intends to offer fifty (50) fellowships every year for working in ICMR Institutes/Centers with state-of-the-art R&D facilities. What is the maximum age criterion to apply under the scheme? The maximum age limit is 32 years, relaxable up to 5 years in case of SC/ST/OBC/PH and women candidates, as on the last date of application. What is the criterion of selection? The selection of the “ICMR Centenary Post-Doctoral Fellows” will be made through the interview of short-listed candidates by a specially constituted Selection Committee by DG, ICMR based on their publications, citations, and impact factors. Is there any age relaxation for deserving candidates? Yes, for deserving candidates age can be relaxed maximum up to a maximum of 3 years based on earlier work done, publications, awards, etc. along with the request by the sponsoring Director/Director-in-Charge. Director General, ICMR may consider and relax on the recommendation of the screening committee. What should be the minimum designation of PDF Guide? The PDF Guide should have the minimum designation of Scientist-C grade. How much will the monthly fellowship be given to the eligible fellow? ICMR-PDFs will be paid a consolidated fellowship of ₹65,000 per month plus House Rent Allowance (HRA) as per Govt. of India rule and Non-Practicing allowance (NPA) as admissible. How much will the contingency grant to be paid to the fellow? An annual contingency grant of ₹3,00,000 will also be paid to the eligible fellow. What is the duration of the fellowship? Two years only and extendable for a maximum of one more year based on outstanding performance and recommendations by the Director/Officer-in-Charge of the concerned ICMR Institutes/Centers. How can an applicant apply for the scheme? The applications may be submitted with all relevant documents to Director General, Sh. Kishor Toppo, Technical Officer-C, Division of Human Resource Development, Indian Council of Medical Research Headquarters, Ansari Nagar, New Delhi-110029. faqs.question: What is the “ICMR- Post Doctoral Fellowship” Scheme?
faqs.answer: ICMR- Post Doctoral Fellowship (ICMR-PDF) Scheme is being instituted to foster high-quality research opportunities for promising fresh Ph. D./MD/MS holders in the cutting-edge areas of basic science, communicable and non-communicable diseases, and reproductive health including nutrition at ICMR Institutes /Centers.

faqs.question: Which is the implementing agency of this scheme?
faqs.answer: Indian Council of Medical Research, Government of India

faqs.question: Who are eligible to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Fresh PhDs/MD/MS within three years of completing their Ph. D./MD/MS degree (awarded) as on the last date of the application and also those who have received their provisional Ph. D./MD/MS degree can apply.

faqs.question: What is the number of fellowships awarded annually?
faqs.answer: ICMR intends to offer fifty (50) fellowships every year for working in ICMR Institutes/Centers with state-of-the-art R&D facilities.

faqs.question: What is the maximum age criterion to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The maximum age limit is 32 years, relaxable up to 5 years in case of SC/ST/OBC/PH and women candidates, as on the last date of application.

faqs.question: What is the criterion of selection?
faqs.answer: The selection of the “ICMR Centenary Post-Doctoral Fellows” will be made through the interview of short-listed candidates by a specially constituted Selection Committee by DG, ICMR based on their publications, citations, and impact factors.

faqs.question: Is there any age relaxation for deserving candidates?
faqs.answer: Yes, for deserving candidates age can be relaxed maximum up to a maximum of 3 years based on earlier work done, publications, awards, etc. along with the request by the sponsoring Director/Director-in-Charge. Director General, ICMR may consider and relax on the recommendation of the screening committee.

faqs.question: What should be the minimum designation of PDF Guide?
faqs.answer: The PDF Guide should have the minimum designation of Scientist-C grade.

faqs.question: How much will the monthly fellowship be given to the eligible fellow?
faqs.answer: ICMR-PDFs will be paid a consolidated fellowship of ₹65,000 per month plus House Rent Allowance (HRA) as per Govt. of India rule and Non-Practicing allowance (NPA) as admissible.

faqs.question: How much will the contingency grant to be paid to the fellow?
faqs.answer: An annual contingency grant of ₹3,00,000 will also be paid to the eligible fellow.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the fellowship?
faqs.answer: Two years only and extendable for a maximum of one more year based on outstanding performance and recommendations by the Director/Officer-in-Charge of the concerned ICMR Institutes/Centers.

faqs.question: How can an applicant apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The applications may be submitted with all relevant documents to Director General, Sh. Kishor Toppo, Technical Officer-C, Division of Human Resource Development, Indian Council of Medical Research Headquarters, Ansari Nagar, New Delhi-110029.
